California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 26, 1988

William L. Corry
P.O. Box 795
Running Springs, CA 92382

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-88-035

Dear Mr. Corry:

We have received your letter regarding the Arrowbear Park
County Water District Board of Directors placing a tax override
issue on the November ballot without the input from property
owners.

The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to
interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act. 1/ The
Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some
of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions,
disclosure of assets and income of public officials,
disqualification of public officials from decisions which could
affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the
activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on
public officials.

The question you pose in your letter is not covered by the
Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on this
matter. In reviewing the material you submitted, it appears
that you may be concerned about possible violations of the
Brown Act. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your
questions to your county district attorney.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[illegible]

By: Jeanette Turvill
Legal Assistant

JET:jaj

_______________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Jan. 9, 1988
Arrowbear Lake[in 14 £ 51]T 00
Ca. 92382


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street Ste. 800
Sacramento,
Ca. 95814

Dear Sirs;

I am writing this letter in the hope of getting information on how to
correct a problem that I see in this area. It concerns taxation without
representation.

The area is a community, Arrowbear Lake, Ca. It consists of approximately
900 homes. The Arrowbear Park County Water District is a self governing
Special District, with powers of water,sewer,and fire. It has a five man
Board of Directors, elected by the registered voters of the District.
There are about 240 registered voters, declared primary homes. This
leaves about 750 homes with no vote on issues of their taxes or water &
Sewer rates.

I am enclosing copies of the minutes of the Districts (#1) May, 1987 and
(#2) June, 1987 meetings. The minutes will show the Board of Directors
did authorize a tax over-ride to be put on the November ballot and did
not notify all taxpayer - property owners in time for their input or
desires of need for same. I have highlighted the items of my concern
which mainly had to do with notification of all taxpayers. However, with
the response that the Committee for Fair Taxation received I feel the
possibility of a vote for all taxpayers in this type of District should
be pursued. To show you the type of response see the enclosed newspaper
clippings.

Any advice or help you can give me in regard to this matter will be
appreciated. This Board has been notorious from it,s beginning for
playing the surprise. They have telephone or one on one conversations
reaching a concensus and then spring it at a Public meeting and not have
much in the way of discussion. To point this out I am enclosing Copies
of the March 14,1986 (#3) meeting Which I think is self explanatory

I thank you for whatever help or advice you can give me.

Yours truly,

Jan. 9, 1988
Arrowbear Lake, [illegible] 2 51 PM 08
Ca. 92382

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street Ste. 800
Sacramento,
Ca. 95814

Dear Sirs;
I am writing this letter in the hope of getting information on how to
correct a problem that I see in this area. It concerns taxation without
representation.
The area is a community, Arrowbear Lake, Ca. It consists of approximately
900 homes. The Arrowbear Park County Water District is a self governing
Special District, with powers of water,sewer,and fire. It has a five man
Board of Directors, elected by the registered voters of the District.
There are about 240 registered voters, declared primary homes. This
leaves about 750 homes with no vote on issues of their taxes or water &
Sewer rates.
I am enclosing copies of the minutes of the Districts (#1) May, 1987 and
(#2) June, 1987 meetings. The minutes will show the Board of Directors
did authorize a tax over-ride to be put on the November ballot and did
not notify all taxpayer - property owners in time for their input or
desires of need for same. I have highlighted the items of my concern
which mainly had to do with notification of all taxpayers. However, with
the response that the Committee for Fair Taxation received I feel the
possibility of a vote for all taxpayers in this type of District should
be pursued. To show you the type of response see the enclosed newspaper
clippings.
Any advice or help you can give me in regard to this matter will be
appreciated. This Board has been notorious from it,s beginning for
playing the surprise. They have telephone or one on one conversations
reaching a concensus and then spring it at a Public meeting and not have
much in the way of discussion. To point this out I am enclosing Copies
of the March 14,1986 (#3) meeting Which I think is self explanatory
I thank you for whatever help or advice you can give me.
Yours truly,

[signature]

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


January 15, 1988


William L. Corry
P.O. Box 795
Running Springs, CA  92382

Re:  88-035

Dear Mr. Corry:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 14, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Jeanette Turvill, in the Legal
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, we will contact you
shortly to advise you as to the information needed. If your
request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as
quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal.
Adm. Code Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

ARROWBEAR PARK COUNTY WATER DISTRICT
Regular Meeting
May 8, 1987

The regular meeting of the Board of Directors of the Arrowbear Park County
Water District was held May 8, 1987 at the Water Office 1383 Park Lane,
Arrowbear Lake, California. The Vice President opened the meeting at the
hour of 7:30 p.m.

Directors in attendance were, Arne Bjaanes, Monta Hammer, John Valcarlis,
Max McIlravy and Baxter Williams. Others in attendance were, Manager
Wymer, John Earl, Richard Steinberg, Sheila Wymer, Nic Radu, Rich
Franzen, Mr. and Mrs. Frank Reilla, Mr. & Mrs. Niva Taylor and Joan Mosley
Solazo.

The Consent Agenda was presented for approval.

Director Hammer moved to accept the Consent Agenda as presented, seconded
by Director Medearis. The motion to accept the Consent Agenda was passed,
Directors Bjaanes absent at this time, Director McIlravy abstained.
Directors in favor thereof, Directors Hammer, Medearis and Williams.

Manager Wymer presented Resolution 87-3-13, A RESOLUTION GRANDFATHERING
SICK LEAVE, VACATION, AND COMPENSATING TIME PRIOR TO ADOPTION OF DISTRICT
POLICIES DATED SEPTEMBER 14, 1984. At this time Manager Wymer suggested
that this item be tabled due to the short period of time the Board had had
to review the material. This item was tabled to the June meeting.

Manager Wymer broached the subject of Goals and Objectives and suggested
that this item be tabled to the June meeting due to the heavy agenda. This
item was also tabled.

Manager Wymer passed out a package of information on annexation, and
suggested that we wait to get the responses from the property owners
involved. Director Hammer asked what happens if everyone agrees to the
annexation? Manager Wymer replied that then we proceed with the rest of
all parties involved insure, the filing charge is $150.00; if there is one
hold-out, then the fee is $700.00. We do not however, need the property
owners consent to annex.

Director McIlravy remarked that this does not include two of the camps, and
asked why not go for these as well? Manager Wymer replied that this area
is what the Board had directed him to proceed on. Director Williams
interjectted that this package is what the Board had decided on, if you want
to change your mind, now is the time to do it. Director McIlravy stated
that he had no objections to this.

Joan Mosley remarked that she had heard that Green Valley Lake was also
trying to annex this same property. Manager Wymer replied that he had
heard the same thing and had called Don Songer, head of CSA-79. There is
nothing to this rumor, the fire department is trying to get Ponderosa
Pines. Manager Wymer also called Mr. Rody at LAFC and no paper work has
been filed. Manager Wymer asked Mr. Rody to contact him if there were any

proceedings on this.

John Egan went over his report with the Board. Mr. Egan has been with
Structural Lab concerning the missing tank results, three of them they just
don't have. Mr. Egan has been working on the annexation and bond splits.
Mr. Egan has been in contact with DNR, and they said they will help
plant once it is in operation, however, they will not help with the
construction costs. Mr. Egan informed the Board that the best place for
the radon tower would be at a place by the road, and the estimated cost
of the radon plant construction and operation is
$138,000.00. Mr. Egan stated that there is time to deal with the radon
problem, and that he would be more concerned as a uranium problem as it
tied to the uranium problem as this time. Manager Wymer remarked that we
are also looking at the chlorination problem, we may have to relocate the
chlorination process at a later date to the location of the aeration tower.

Mr. Strong informed the Board that he was in the process of drafting the
complaint on Herm Gruenholz for a meeting. Mr. Strong passed out a draft of "Personnel Policy" with respect
to PLSA. Manager Wymer remarked that there were only two personel
involved, these are in the water and sewer departments. We were in a problem
with this, we are only trying to satisfy PLSA. Mr. Strong suggested that
the board take this under advisement, he was not sure how many positions
were to be included, and if the five department was to be included in this.
Director Williams asked Mr. Strong, "as it stands then at this point, you
will be filing some type of action on the collection of the past due bill
that has been in question now for some months? W. Strong replied, yes,
we will be filing a specific act in the Justice Court in Twin Parks.

Director Williams, Chairman of the Finance Committee presented the proposed
budget for the fiscal year 1987-1988. There is a proposed increase in the
standby water and sewer charges from the old $10.00 per parcel to $20.00
per parcel in water and $25.00 per parcel in sewer. Also there is a
proposed increase of water meter connection fees to $900.00, and an increase
on sewer permits to $1,600.00. There is a proposed increase of the monthly
sewer service charge of $1.50. The fire and board budgets have no major
changes in them.

Director Williams remarked that there will be a $4,700.00 overrun in the
current years sewer budgets. We propose to make a internal loan from the
sewer replacement fund to balance this. In addition to this, our share of
the Running Springs plant expansion will be $30,600.00 during the coming
fiscal year. We propose to obtain the $30,600.00 through a internal loan
from a yet to be determined source fund account.

At the hour of 8:25 p.m. Vice President Williams declared the Public
Hearing open, and turned the gavel over to President Bjaanes.

Wes Ray stated that he had no objection to the increases, most agencies are
going to the $30.00 standby charges. Mr. Ray suggested raising the standby
charges on both water and sewer to the full $30.00, and not raise the sewer
service charge by the $1.50. President Bjaanes remarked that the thought
behind this is that the Board is trying to make each department self
sufficient.

#2

ARROWBEAR PARK COUNTY WATER DISTRICT
Regular Meeting
June 12, 1977

The regular meeting of the Board of Directors of the Long Cane Tr. County
of a Division was held June 12, 1977 at the Water Office, 540 Fox Farm
Lakewood, Lake, California. The President called the meeting open at 2:00 P.M.

Directors in attendance were, Dave Bilinski, Wayne Hammer, Tom Valencia,
Don Weatherby and Shelton. Others in attendance were, John
Jones, John Gray, Richard Strong, Shellie Vance, Mr. & Mrs. Carl Gray, Jr.
Mrs. Max Briskie, Yvonne Taylor, Virginia Boone, Mr. & Mrs. Tina D. [illegible]
Mr. & Mrs. George Secombre, Mr. & Mrs. Pearl Young, Mr. [illegible] and
Stephens, Ralph Franzen, and Mr. & Mrs. Roger Bones.

The Consent Agenda was presented for approval.

Alfowned Williams moved to accept the finance agenda as presented, including
A Arrowbear Annex. Motion carried.

At this time Director Williams, chairman of the finance committee reported
on the finding of the review of the Local Agency Investment Fund.

Director Medearis moved to authorize the investment of district funds into
the Local Agency Investment Fund, to be marked as District Funds and to
ongoing review and analysis of the earning of that fund. Seconded by
Director McIlravy. Motion carried by a unanimous vote of the Board.

President Biannies introduced Resolution 27-6-12-77, A RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING
INVESTMENT OF ARROWBEAR PARK COUNTY WATER DISTRICT MONIES IN LOCAL AGENCY
INVESTMENT FUNDS. President Biannies stated that two signatures would
authorize deposits or withdrawals of funds should be required; and that the
should be that of the President of the Board and the Chairman of the
Finance Committee. After discussion,

Director McIlravy moved to adopt Resolution 27-6-12-77 with the provisions
that the Chairman of the Board be authorized to make such transfers and
to make all necessary transfer of funds. Motion carried by a unanimous
vote of the Board.

Director Williams continued with his report, in that the consensus of the
Committee is that the Manager should report on investments on a quarterly
basis to the Board, this process to begin in September.

Director Williams remarked that the tax over-ride will be divyed under a
three-band water rate. However review was presented at this time,
Firm, and of the opinion at this time, and recommending that we file for
place this item on the November ballot. It is the consensus of opinion
that all alternatives such as site, funding, etc. have not been thorougly
explored. The actual proposal is not clearly defined to the extent vi can
comfortable placing it before the voters.

Williams broached the subject of the proposed Ft. across the third poleary
increases. This is in place in the budget and is of for adoption wight.
The total cost of the water to the District will run high with
excluded benefits, retirement, etc. The organization of the committee is
not This be late in the budget. This matter is of essential to the
Board committee for discussion. It is to be suggested possibly
prayed as a across the board increase, some form of merit raise, just
look at cost of living; there is no recommendation on this in this point.

Director Williams approached water standby charges. The committee had
recommended that a increase from $1.00 to $30.00 is in the proposed
budget. The committee is recommending a change to $1.00 from $10.00.
$10.00. This would raise a additional $11,000.00. This fund would go to
the Master Plan Reserve.

Director Williams approached the subject of budget adjustment and
suggested that these transfers be made within departments, with any large
adjustments coming back to the board following a finance committee
meeting.

Director Hammer moved to authorize the secretary to make the necessary
budget transfers to close the books, these to come back for final approval
at the July meeting. Seconded by Director McIlravy. Motion carried by a
unanimous vote of the board.

Director Williams informed the Board that the miscellaneous items are the
selection of an auditor at a later time on this agenda. The Danger will
give a report on the fund accounts, and balances in those accounts in
September.

Director Williams called for a finance committee meeting June 27, 1987 at
the hour of 8:30 a.m.

At the hour of 8:00 p.m. the regular meeting was closed to a Public Hearing

Manager Wymer pointed out that this was the second public hearing on the
budget, the only change is the recommendation on the change of the water
standby charge from $10.00 to $30.00.

Agnes Secombe asked if the standby charges were by parcel or lot? Manager
Wymer replied by parcel. Mrs. Calkins asked about the CLAWA standby
charges. Mrs. Calkins also asked about the $10,000.00 park fund we
stated that, where it would be very nice to have the park, she felt that
those funds could be better used in the water department.

At the hour of 8:15 p.m. the Public Hearing was closed. The regular
meeting was re-opened at the hour of 8:30 p.m.

Resolution 87-6-12, a RESOLUTION AMENDING RESOLUTION 76-11-12-A APPROVING
AND ADOPTING RULES AND REGULATIONS, SEWER SERVICE was presented for
approval. This allows the sewer service charges to be raised from $9.00
per month to $10.50 per month.

Director Williams moved to approve Resolution 87-6-12, seconded by Director

is Harris. Motion carried by unanimous vote of the Board.

Resolution 87-6-12-A, A RESOLUTION OF THE ARROHEAD PARK JOINTLY OWNED
DISTRICT AUTHORIZING WATER STANDBY CHARGES ON UNIMPROVED LOTS, was
presented for approval. Discussion was brought forth in whether this would
be raised to $20.00 per lot and water resolution number will be held in
service either date $20.00 increase or the $10.00 increase).

Director Hammer moved to accept Resolution 87-6-12-A as presented for
REVIEW to $30.00. The motion was by [illegible] A Director, Directors Hess.
Motion carried.

Director Williams moved to accept Resolution 87-6-12-B with the amount to
be raised to $10.00 to $20.00 be presented by Director Williams with Directors
Libroei, Hammer, Medsaris, McIlravy and Williams. President Bjanes No.
Motion carried.

Resolution 87-6-12-B, A RESOLUTION OF THE ARROHEAD PARK JOINTLY OWNED
DISTRICT AUTHORIZING SEWER STANDBY CHARGES ON UNIMPROVED LOTS, was presented for
approval. This would raise the sewer standby charges from $5.00 per lot to
$7.50 per lot.

Motion by Director Williams to accept Resolution 87-6-12-B as presented,
seconded by Director McIlravy. Directors in favor [illegible], Directors
Medsaris, McIlravy and Williams. Director Hammer No. Motion carried.

Resolution 87-6-12-C, A RESOLUTION AMENDING RESOLUTION 82-7-9-E APPROVING
AND ADOPTING RULES AND REGULATIONS, SEWER SERVICE, was presented for
approval. This would raise the sewer permits and fee from $300.00 to
$7,600.00.

Director McIlravy moved to accept Resolution 87-6-12-C as presented,
seconded by Director Williams. Motion passed by a unanimous vote of the
Board.

Resolution 87-6-12-D, A RESOLUTION AMENDING RESOLUTION 82-7-9-E APPROVING
AND ADOPTING RULES AND REGULATIONS, WATER SERVICE, was presented for
approval. This would raise the water installation charge from $100.00 to
$500.00.

Director McIlravy moved to accept Resolution 87-6-12-D as presented,
seconded by Director Williams. Motion passed by a unanimous vote of the
Board.

At this time the budget for the fiscal year 1987-1988 was presented for
approval.

Director Medsaris moved to accept the budget as presented, seconded by
Director Williams. Director Hammer asked how you can allocate salary
increases and not grant them? President Bjanes replied that if
salaries increases are not granted, than the surplus funds will be freed to
other accounts. This will be decided at the July meeting. At this point
Director Hammer remarked "that he could see better places for the $8." The
budget was adopted by a unanimous vote of the Board.

At this time Mr. Richard Anderson, financial consultant from Rogers,
Arkansas Melody and Scott gave his initial review of the assessment
account. Mr. Anderson asked Vaughn Wymer several questions with regard to
what was rated back to the Manager Wymer explained he had not received
having seen the report prior to this meeting but he noticed going back was
years, without some research he could not answer these questions at this
time. Mr. Anderson recommended that the business revenue be reviewed again from
Johnson in regard to the district missing his obligations to Farmers Home
Administration. President Bjaanes asked, given the current status of the
account, what if we do not get our obligation to Farmers Home
Administration? Mr. Anderson replied no.

Manager Wymer asked Mr. Anderson why, when asked in the office how things
looked, he had replied "that in integrity of the books the than very, there
would be no problem meeting the obligations in one year 2007." Mr. Anderson
replied "I very very sorry." Manager Wymer explained that we integrity that
stated "yes you did."

Director Williams asked if the delinquencies are managed, can we meet our
obligations, and do our payments to Farmers Home Administration delinquent
delinquencies, what I am asking is are we delinquent in our payments to FMHA
Farmers Home Administration?" Mr. Anderson replied yes we are, as far as
far as I can tell delinquent in any payment to FMHA on the bonds." Mr.
Anderson further stated that the delinquencies should be handled on a yearly
basis. Manager Wymer stated that until this year, the County has not had
the capability to provide us with a list of delinquencies.

President Bjaanes - 1) there are apparent discrepancies between what staff
had generated in numbers and what the financial consultant has generated in
numbers. These need to be cleared up. 2) the projections that I see here
are that if we don't meeting the delinquencies, we will not be able
to meet our obligation of FMHA. 3) the assessmen account needs to
be put into a method that allows for, check, control and verification on a
month to month basis, we have to get this under control. 4) need to clear
up the advise from general fund, and get it back to real
projections on how to meet the payments. President Bjaanes proposed that
the board members look at the information provided, on the July agenda put
on higher specific actions on how to correct the problem.

Director Williams remarked that he was not sure that this board can form a
plan of action, we need to lean on Mr. Anderson. President Bjaanes
suggested a workshop to better understand this problem. The Board was
told on this suggestion, all agreed to the workshop. This will be held
July 12, 1987 at 9:00 a.m. in the board room. Mr. Anderson will attend
this workshop. Action on any recommendations to be taken at the August
meeting.

At this time Director Williams, Chairman of the Finance Committee broached
the subject of the hiring of a auditor for the district. There are three
candidates, Zulch at $2,900.00, Ellis & Payne at $2,920.00 and Rogers
Anderson, Malody & Scott at $2,000.00 to $4,000.00. President Bjaanes
remarked that as we go into the selection process, we would suggest that
you give serious consideration to the fact that in the course of the past
year, Mr. Anderson has become relatively familiar with how this district

operatas, with its book, and its problems. I would suggest with that kind
experience, I would strongly recommend that we confer with directions firm out
the Scobie.

Director McIlravy moved to accept Rogers, Anderson, Valarik & Doan in the
capacity as District Engineers and Directors Williams. District carried by
a unanimous vote of the Board.

Manager Wymer presented Resolution 87-5-13, A RESOLUTION GRANTING
SICK LEAVE, VACATION, AND COMPENSATING TIME PRIOR TO ADOPTION OF DISTRICT
POLICIES DATED SEPTEMBER 14, 1984.

Director Williams moved to accept Resolution 87-5-12, the motion died for
lack of a second.

Director remarked that you have before you a issue that has been
raised by the Board, it is something the Board wants to ID something about.
What do you want to do? Do you want to reject it, study it, table it,
or? Director Bennet stated that he DID in the past, that with the
rest of the board was asked for a spirit, the reply was Director
Williams, Adept, Director Nadearis, don't know, Director Hammer, don't
know, Director McIlravy, see the old policy. Most further discussion.

Director McIlravy moved to table this matter to the July meeting, with a
request from the Finance Committee, seconded by Director Wedearis.
President Bjaanes instructed legal counsel to look this over, and provide
any kind of analysis as to legality, options, etc.. Staff to prepare
arguments pro or con. Motion carried by a unanimous vote of the Board.

Manager Wymer suggested that the discussion on goals and objectives be
tabled to the July meeting. The Board concures with this.

Manager Wymer presented Resolution 87-5-14, A RESOLUTION OF APPLICATION BY
THE WEST PORK COUNTY WATER DISTRCT REQUESTING THE SOIL CONSERVATION
COMMISSION TO TAKE PROCEEDINGS FOR THE ANNEXATION OF LAND TO THE NORTH OF
THE DISTRICT. Manager Wymer informed the Board that he has had only one
& from a property owner requesting annexation. That being,
the Boy Scouts, our sphere of influence splits their property in half.
They do not want the property split, and suggested that we annex the entire
portion of their land. President Bjaanes recommended that the Board adopt two
resolutions for annexation despite the opposition from the camp with
the provision that this Board meet with the property owner and have a
plan in mind. As soon as the annexation is completed, so far a
expansion of the sphere of influence to include the entire property in
question.

Director McIlravy asked about Ponderosa Pines. Ponderosa Pines his
educated that they want to be annexed into our sphere, the camp is not in
the proposed annexation at this time.

Director McIlravy moved to pass Resolution 87-5-8 as modified, seconded by
Director Wedearis. (the modification to include Ponderosa Pines and
Driven Valley. Manager Wymer pointed out that this will cause the maps to
have to be redrawn, and additional annexation fees) Motion carried by a
unanimous vote of the Board.

Manager Wymer broached the subject of additional items that he has brought
before the Board before. While the Board has tabled some items, some of the old ones remain unresolved. I would like to point out that from time to time I have requested the need for power at the lift station;
the drive way needs to be repaired, and the need of a new roof for the old fire house. Of these things are a matter. "I want the Board to be
aware that when it considers priority items, these things must be included,"
President Bjaanes instructed Manager Wymer to include items of this nature
as an item of old business.

John Egan went over his monthly report. Mr. Egan presented
the Union-Stalder, A RESOLUTION DIRECTING MAKING OF AMENDED ASSESSMENT.

Director Williams moved to pass resolution directing as presented
by Director Madearis. Motion carried by a unanimous vote of the Board.

Director Williams moved to set the time for a Public Hearing on Sundial
easements for July 10, 1987 at 8:00 p.m., seconded by Director Madearis.
Motion carried by a unanimous vote of the Board.

Mr. Egan informed the Board that he will prepare a resolution for a
Preliminary Environmental Assessment for the July meeting. Chief Taylor
asked Mr. Egan if anyone at Environmental Health has a site approval?
Chief Taylor stated that you will be storing hazardous material. Mr. Egan
will check on the requirement on this.

Mr. Strong informed the Board that with reference to Mr. Gruenholz, he will
file suite in the Twin Peaks Court on June 15, 1987. Mr. Strong suggested
that the Board adopt the Policy to comply with ELSA. President Bjaanes
said if the District falls under the ELSA? Mr. Strong replied that his
feeling if it was challenged in Federal Court we would probably come under
it. He seems to be the criteria. "If you have to that I implore to get
on this. President Bjaanes deferred this item to the July meeting, and
instructed Mr. Strong to have a determination as to whether this district
falls within the rain of the ELSA.

Chief Taylor informed the Board that there had been a total of 18 calls for
the month of May. 6 medical aids, 4 mutual aids, 1 illegal fire, 1 smoke
check, 1 investigation, and 5 wildland fires. The water tender was used on
two of the wildland fires by the Forest Service.

Chief Taylor informed the Board that on June 16 there is a augmentation
meeting. The District has two men that will attend a over the side crews.
Chief Strong stated that over a year ago he had asked for a personal
policy for the position of Chief. To date, there has been nothing done on
this. President Bjaanes instructed Director Madearis, chairman of that
committee to get this policy under way.

Chief Taylor stated that several months ago he had requested responses from
people in the community who had property that could be suitable for a fire
station. To date, Mr. Harich has been the only one to respond. The total
price of the building and property is $295,000.00. Chief Taylor passed out

a package with a proposal for the Harich property on Highway 18 along with two plans in connection with this. Chief Taylor stated that if a garage of $45.00 per improved property and $10.00 per unimproved property were placed you would have a total of $9,050.00 per year. Chief Taylor stated that the Harich property does not have to be tied in the tax over-ride. With the money left after the annual payment on the building, you could apply it to the Fire Department to help carry out the problem of the equipment being outside. At the end of the 10-year period, you could build a new station or you could sell the property and use the money to go down in taxes or take action in the registered voters in Arrowhead, and they are in favor of this tax situation.

President Bjaanes stated that "you are proposing a tax over-ride in place on the November Ballot, and that the charges be $45.00 per improved property, and $10.00 per unimproved property." President Bjaanes asked for a card comment.

Director McIlvary stated that he had attended an organization meeting with a group of Taylor, and if we cursalves, they are in favor to help if. If the voters want this, they will vote for it, it should be on the ballot.

Director Hammer agreed, give it to the people for a vote. If does not tie us to the Harich property.

Director Mederis, Put it on the ballot.

Director Williams remarked that he had a problem with the 45/10 ratio.

President Bjaanes remarked that there was no question of the need for additional personnel, equipment and a building, the question is in procedure.

At this time Yvonne Taylor submitted a petition signed by registered voters in favor of the over-ride to the Board. President Bjaanes asked who circulated the petition; Mrs. Taylor replied herself and Mrs. Bristol. Mrs. Wymer stated that the Board and Bristol were of the opinion this discussion would be placed on the billing. This was not done. Mrs. Wymer asked how long this charge would be on? Chief Taylor replied, indefinitely. Mrs. Wymer asked if the property owners would have a say in the decision, or just the registered voters? The reply was just the registered voters.

President Bjaanes raised the question, if the newly acquired property being annexed will be a part of this, or will there have to be another ballot measure for over-ride. Mr. Strong replied that the annexation is not completed by the November election, then that portion will be some from this over-ride. President Bjaanes felt that we should wait till the completion of the annexation before we put this on the ballot. In a long range plan, this is the only logical way to do. Director McIlvary felt that this was wasting time, we should move now. President Bjaanes reiterated, there was not disagreement on the need for the taxation, the question is in the timing of the placement on the election. Mrs. Wymer asked what would be the next time if this does not go on the November [illegible]

It is moved by Director Bjaanes replied that this could be June 22 of a mail
ballot. After further discussion,

Director McIntyre moved to open the meeting on the November
[illegible] seconded by Director Mederis, Directors in favor charge;
[illegible], Mederis and Hammer. Opposed, Directors Bryant and Williams
motion carried.

Mr. February asked Board authorization to attend a election seminar, this
is June 22, 1957, and is on her day off and will involve overtime.

Director Mederis moved to authorize attendance and time for oversight of a
[illegible] seconded by Director Williams. Motion carried by a unanimous vote
of the Board.

Mr. Tucker gave up data on the C.C.A.F. organization progress. Parks &
Recreation are very interested in purchasing the park and suggested is
Snowbear Water District could put some funds toward the purchase of the
park. They might have a day in the way it was run.

President Bjaanes suggested development of a proposed contract between
the District and Parks and Recreation Department. Everyone agreed they would like
to see the park remain, however, this district is presently problems now
with regard to the expenditure of funds in that direction.

President Bjaanes informed the Board that the contract with the attorney is
due for renewal in September, and suggested that they all look this over
and come back with recommendations at the August meeting.

There being no further business, the President declared the meeting closed
at the hour of 12:15 a.m.

The next regular meeting will be July 13, 1957

APPROVED:

                                          _____________________
                                               Secretary


Vinus Bjaanes, President

ARROWBEAR PARK COUNTY WATER DISTRICT
Regular Meeting

The regular meeting of the Board of Directors of the Arrowbear Park County
Water District was held March 14, 1986 at the Water Office, 2365 Fir Drive,
Arrowbear Lake, California. The President declared the meeting open at the
hour of 7:30 p.m.

Directors in attendance were, Arne Bjaanes, Monte Hammer, John Medearis,
Max McIiravy and Baxter Williams. Others in attendance were, Byron Finley,
Donald Julius, Sheila Waymer, Mr. Slusher, Dennis, Whittington Paul
Stroughton, Mr. & Mrs. Norm Taylor, Bill Carlisle, Jr. Van Berckelaer, Joan
Boles, Al Mellenger and Mr. & Mrs. J. K. Johnson.

President Bjaanes stated that at this time there is no set policy for
payment of special meeting, and submitted a draft policy for consideration.
President Bjaanes felt that the last special meeting should be a paid
meeting due to the time it involved. After Board discussion this item was
added to the consent agenda.

The Consent Agenda was presented for approval. Director Hammer moved to
accept the Consent Agenda as presented, seconded by Director McIiravy,
after discussion, Directors, Williams, Hammer and McIiravy voted to approve
the consent agenda. Director Medearis opposed. Motion carried.

President Bjaanes stated "at this point in time he would like to take the
privilege of bypassing the agenda for a short while. The key in any
relationship of a board and the attorney is a high level of confidence.
I think that has deteriorated, and in discussions with Board members
individually there is a great deal of dissatisfaction with the relationship
between the attorney and the Board. I think there is a time when a change
is necessary and a change will improve the way the Board functions, with
that I will open this for Board discussion." Director McIiravy moved to
get a new attorney, seconded by Director Williams. Director Hammer stated
"that he had no idea you were going to do this tonight. I realize we did
talk last month; are we going to vote on this without hearing the reasons?"
Mr. Finley pointed out that he had a right to a closed session on this. As
a employee of the district I have a right to a closed session, to have
advanced notice, and to meet with the Board on this. I have had no notice
at all, this is the first time I have heard anything about this. I have
been a employee of this District for 18 years. President Bjaanes remarked
that he believed that the proper term was not employee, but consultant to
the Board. President Bjaanes stated that the reason this has come about is
that in discussions I have had with individual Board members, the concerns
I seem to feel is that a termination is important. At this time a vote was
called for. Directors in factor thereof, Williams, Medearis, McIiravy;
Opposed Hammer. Motion carried.

President Bjaanes stated that he was concerned about the District being
without legal counsel and asked if there were any recommendations.
Director McIiravy recommended that Mr. Bill Carlisle present the District
on a interim basis until we decide who we are going to get. President

Bjaanes remarked that he was concerned about having a attorney on board. I believe that the task of legal council is something that needs to be brought up for discussion and needs to be placed for on open bid with proposals made from legal council in regard to qualifications. In the mean time we need legal council on board. I have spoken with Mr. Carlisle and discussed the possibility of him representing us, and I feel comfortable with that as a interim solution with a search mounted for a legal council. That search obviously will not prevent Mr. Carlisle from seeking the position.

Mr. Carlisle remarked that he had been asked here this evening to comment as legal council necessary to the Board. I am not sure that I personally would want to come on as permanent legal council. President Bjaanes remarked that Mr. Carlisle had remarked to him that on a interim basis his fees are $75.00 per hour with a 6 hour minimum. Mr. Finley pointed out that his agreement with the District indicated that it can be terminated by either party upon 30 day notice. At this time Director McIlravy moved to appoint Mr. Carlisle as interim legal council to the Board effect ively immediately at the rate of 6 hour minimum at $75.00 per hour. seconded by Director Williams. Mr. Slusher, objected due to a lack of discussion. Director Hammer stated that he was strongly opposed to what was going on, he did not feel that we need two attorneys at the same time. if Byron is going to be employed by the District for another month, why do we need additional council? I get the impression that this was pre-planned. I am not against this. I stressed this with you and you knew my feelings. I get the direct impression that this was pre-planned. Mr. Carlisle is here. President Bjaanes replied that's right. I talked with the Board Members individually to get their perception as to what they felt was necessary on the best interest of the District. With respect to two attorneys on board, I feel that for the duration of the period that Mr. Finley will be paid is bound by contract, however, if do you want the new council on board to get familiar with the District. Directors in favor of the motion on the floor, Williams, Medearis, McIlravy, opposed, Director Hammer. Motion Carried.

Director Hammer suggested that Mr. Finley be moved up on the agenda to enable him to give his report, and then leave. Mr. Finley reported that he had been instructed to research does and don'ts for a director. At this time Mr. Finley stated that one of the items of the Brown Act is that you can't do what you just did, that is to predetmine what will be the result of a meeting outside of the public meeting. It is obvious by having another attorney here, and by bring this up in the manner it was brought up there was a consensus and a decision made of what would be done at a meeting before the meeting; that is in violation of the Brown Act.

Mr. Finley read several pages on the function of the Board in relation with Staff. The President asked me just before the last board meeting to comment on the does and don'ts of what a Board Member can do. No individual Board Member has the authority to authorize other people to act, to hire, or to research or contact others and us _______ of the Board or District unless there is pre-authorization from [illegible] 53022 of the Water Code, 31000 of the County Water Code, 31001 [illegible] ZFPCC-12 were all referenced by Mr. Finley indicating that the District should take a position as to whether individuals will be allowed to do research in the name of the district. Mr. Finley informed the Board that a County Water

District derives its powers from the legislature. Section 31545 states that
the Board may appoint officers other than the engineer with compensation.
Section 330 of the Penal Code talks about false impersonation not
only of police and firemen, but other people with government duties.

Mr. Slusher stated that it appeared to him that Mr. Finley was being rail-
roaded without cause. I have not heard one word of cause as to why Mr.
Finley is being fired.

President Bjaanes replied that certain officers serve at the pleasure of
the Board. A high level of respect and confidence is essential. With
respect to the violation of the Brown Act, there is a section that states
there shall be no agreements outside a board meeting. That does not
include discussions between two board members, nor does it conclude that
considerations can be given to possible decisions made on any binding level until a motion was made in the content of the board
meeting. I would hope that each Board Member voted as a individual. Mr.
Finley stated that staff has copies of all my materials, I will be happy to
meet with anyone, but at District expense. Mr. Finley stated that he has
been very happy in his 18 years with the District. Mr. Slusher asked asked
if there would be Board Members now making separate contracts? President
Bjaanes, replied no, no single Board Member can act individually.

At this time President Bjaanes presented Certificates for the Bishop Oil
Fire School to firemen Whittington Stroughton, J.R. Johnston and Kevin
Cooke.

Donald Julius acting for Manager Wymer informed the Board that this had
been a quiet month. On the uranium treatment problem we have checked with
Bill Gedney of State Health as to any word from Sacramento. Mr. Gedney
indicates that they are working, and hopes to have something in 2 1/2
weeks. We are still receiving correspondence on the state mandate.

Mr. Julius stated that you received status reports on our activity with
seven different firms. Please consider this as privileged information, it
should not go beyond the Board. Several of these companies have not
responded to your inquires. L.A. Water Treatment's test cylinder did not
prove effective and has been removed. Applied Filtronics is requesting
$1,600.00 to place a test unit, after discussion with Manager Wymer, I
would recommend against this. The more we get into this the more we find
that the removal of uranium for domestic water use is almost a quandry.
President Bjaanes interjected that when IWT was contacted they responded
with a package of information, has anything like this been received from
Applied Filtronics? Mr. Julius replied no. Mr. Julius informed the Board
that IWT has placed a test cartridge in the office. February 19th samples
tested showed a gross alpha reduction from 36.7 to 30 pico curies, we feel
this was a good test. The February 26th showed a reduction from the
same 36.7 to 8/10th pico curies. It appears that this process is effective,
there have been another set of tests taken. IWT and Press Cotter
Writing Co. further as they made indicated they would need to get
something in writing as to the disposal. President Bjaanes asked if Cotter
will take the waste? Mr. Julius replied if we go with ITA and the sulfuric
acid regeneration, yes, Filtronics was not reported in the previous
report, but they were contacted two years ago. They are no longer in a

QUESTION: s to your county district attorney. 
JET: jaj
embedding.first_500_words: / California Fair Political Practices Commission January 26, 1988 William L. Corry P.O. Box 795 Running Springs, CA 92382 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-88-035 Dear Mr. Corry: We have received your letter regarding the Arrowbear Park County Water District Board of Directors placing a tax override issue on the November ballot without the input from property owners. The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act. 1/ The Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions, disclosure of assets and income of public officials, disqualification of public officials from decisions which could affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on public officials. The question you pose in your letter is not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on this matter. In reviewing the material you submitted, it appears that you may be concerned about possible violations of the Brown Act. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your questions to your county district attorney. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel [illegible] By: Jeanette Turvill Legal Assistant JET:jaj _______________________ 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 Jan. 9, 1988 Arrowbear Lake[in 14 £ 51]T 00 Ca. 92382 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street Ste. 800 Sacramento, Ca. 95814 Dear Sirs; I am writing this letter in the hope of getting information on how to correct a problem that I see in this area. It concerns taxation without representation. The area is a community, Arrowbear Lake, Ca. It consists of approximately 900 homes. The Arrowbear Park County Water District is a self governing Special District, with powers of water,sewer,and fire. It has a five man Board of Directors, elected by the registered voters of the District. There are about 240 registered voters, declared primary homes. This leaves about 750 homes with no vote on issues of their taxes or water & Sewer rates. I am enclosing copies of the minutes of the Districts (#1) May, 1987 and (#2) June, 1987 meetings. The minutes will show the Board of Directors did authorize a tax over-ride to be put on the November ballot and did not notify all taxpayer - property owners in time for their input or desires of need for same. I have highlighted the items of my concern which mainly had to do with notification of all taxpayers. However, with the response that the Committee for Fair Taxation received I feel the possibility of a vote for all taxpayers in this type of District should be pursued. To show you the type of response see the enclosed newspaper clippings. Any advice or help you can give me in regard to this matter will be appreciated. This Board has been
